dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini won the day on Hacker News: Gemma 4 drew a big, mostly positive launch thread with strong hands-on praise despite a few notable bugs [E1].
winnerExplanation: Google Gemini had the strongest positive signal because Gemma 4 was a major front-page story with heavy engagement and an overall favorable tone: commenters praised capabilities, local usability, and image outputs, even while noting some broken behavior in specific variants [E1]. Anthropic also had a good day, but its positivity was more indirect—mainly Claude being treated as a preferred tool or the current standard in comparison threads [E2][E3]. OpenAI's clearest direct signal was negative, with the TBPN acquisition discussed skeptically as a strange or cynical media/PR move [E5]. Microsoft Copilot had no clear, product-specific HN signal in the provided stories/comments.

openai.judgementSnippet: Net negative: OpenAI's main direct mention was skepticism around its TBPN acquisition, with commenters worrying about media independence and calling it a weird or cynical move [E5].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Mildly positive: Claude/Claude Code was repeatedly described as a preferred alternative in the Cursor discussion, and Anthropic's latest Opus line was treated as the current bar in Qwen comparisons [E2][E3].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Positive: the Gemma 4 launch got broad, largely upbeat reaction for capability and local performance, with some bugs tempering enthusiasm; Gemini also appeared as a current benchmark target in comparison talk [E1][E4].

evidence.summary: 'Google releases Gemma 4 open models' produced a large, mostly positive discussion: commenters praised capabilities and local performance, though some reported broken behavior in 31B and incorrect reasoning outputs.
In 'Cursor 3,' multiple commenters said Claude Code setups worked better or made Cursor less compelling, giving Anthropic a favorable comparison signal.
In 'Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents,' commenters criticized comparisons against Opus 4.5 instead of current Opus 4.6, implicitly treating Anthropic's latest model as the stronger benchmark.
The same Qwen thread objected to benchmarking against Gemini Pro 3.0 instead of 3.1, implying Gemini 3.1 is the more relevant current standard.
In 'OpenAI Acquires TBPN,' commenters were openly skeptical, raising concerns about media independence, calling the deal odd, and framing it as part of a cynical PR push.
